title: Triple Laundry Basket Organizer
summary: A planked-top frame that holds three laundry baskets at counter height — every dimension in this build is derived from the baskets you actually buy, not a fixed cut list.
description:
Nearly every measurement in this stand is a formula keyed to the baskets you're buying, not a fixed number — the same design philosophy as this catalog's other basket- and mirror-sized organizers. Buy the three baskets FIRST, measure their depth and the inside lip width, then work the frame dimensions from those real numbers. A stand built to a generic cut list and married to baskets that don't quite match will either bind or leave sloppy gaps.

The two end frames are the foundation, and they need to be genuinely square before anything else happens — measure both diagonals and adjust until they match, the same check that matters on any box frame. Once square, the basket cleats (runners) get marked and leveled across both frames before fastening, because a cleat that's slightly higher on one end than the other tips every basket that rests on it.

The top planking closes out the build and is the one place to double-check square again before nailing — small errors in the frame become very visible once a grid of parallel boards sits across the top. If your basket depth measurement lands close to 24", the plan calls out cutting to 23-7/8" instead, which is the kind of quarter-inch that makes the difference between stock cut efficiently from standard lumber lengths and stock that wastes a inch of every board.

tools.note: Every frame and cleat dimension derives from the baskets you actually buy — measure before cutting.

materials.name: 2x4, 10 ft
materials.note: Legs, frame, and basket cleats — estimate; depends on your baskets' dimensions.

materials.name: Laundry baskets, 1.5 bushel
materials.note: Not part of the woodworking cost — buy these FIRST and measure them before cutting anything.

cutList.note: Formula: (your baskets' inside lip measurement + 3") x 3, plus 3" more. About 60" for typical 1.5-bushel baskets.
Cut to your basket's actual depth. If that number lands close to 24", cut to 23-7/8" instead to use lumber efficiently.
Length matches your basket depth; quantity depends on the stand's overall width — 10 to 12 boards is typical.

steps.title: Build the two end frames
steps.body: Build two frames from the outer and inner legs using the longer screws. Check each for square by measuring opposite diagonals and adjusting until they match exactly — everything else in this build references off these two frames.

steps.title: Install the basket cleats
steps.body: Mark the location of the cleats (runners) on both frames at the same height, and attach level inside the frames with two screws per joint. A cleat that's off-level on one end will tip every basket resting on it.

steps.title: Join the frames and plank the top
steps.body:
Attach the long horizontal frame pieces to connect the two end frames into one stand, checking for square again before moving on.

Attach the 1x6 top planking across the frame with the shorter screws, even gaps between boards. Sand, finish, and set the baskets in place.

images.alt: Wood stand holding three laundry baskets side by side under a planked top
